rows.definition: A write-time (post-tool) gate on tool RESULTS that decides if bytes enter context as-is, must be quarantined, or paged out to a pointer.
rows.distinction: Gates RESULT BYTES on the text side - NOT KV-MMU (which evicts K/V spans on the attention side) and NOT recall (the persisted core dump).

rows.definition: The bridge that turns ctxmmu's logical quarantine verdict into a mechanical one by evicting K/V spans from the kernel's attention cache.
rows.distinction: Evicts K/V spans on the ATTENTION side - NOT context-MMU (which gates result bytes on the text side) and NOT the KV cache (the storage it evicts from).

rows.definition: The persisted session core-dump: a page table over a content-addressed swap device, reloadable in a fresh process where a sealed page stays sealed.
rows.distinction: PERSISTENCE of a finished session - NOT compaction (provider prefix reuse on the wire) and NOT context-MMU (the live result gate).

rows.definition: Provider prefix reuse on the wire: re-sending a byte-identical prefix so the provider serves it from its prompt cache instead of re-prefilling.
rows.distinction: REUSE on the wire - NOT recall (persisting a finished session) and NOT context-MMU (gating result bytes into context).
gaps: compaction vs provider prompt-cache reuse is defined-only

rows.definition: The planner that forecasts the bounded resident working set of context spans for a session and learns from witnessed attention outcomes.
rows.distinction: PLANS the resident working set - NOT context-MMU (the per-result admission gate) and NOT recall (the persisted image).
gaps: the ctx* planner family is defined-only, not yet in the glossary
